task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a data model named exactly 'Authentication_Events' in the Splunk 'Data Models' management list or displayed at the top of the Data Model editor.
Look at the root dataset definition in the Data Model editor. The 'Constraints' field must explicitly include 'index=security_logs'.

task_completion.description: Create at least two child datasets representing distinct authentication outcomes
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the dataset hierarchy (usually on the left side of the Data Model editor). There must be at least two indented child datasets under the root dataset (e.g., representing successful and failed logins).

task_completion.description: Define at least three fields across the data model objects
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the fields/attributes list for the datasets in the editor. There must be at least three extracted, calculated, or eval fields added (excluding default Splunk fields like _time, host, source, sourcetype).

task_completion.description: Enable data model acceleration with a summary range of at least one day
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the 'Edit Acceleration' dialog showing the 'Accelerate' checkbox ticked with a Summary Range of '1 Day' (or greater), or look for a yellow lightning bolt icon next to 'Authentication_Events' in the Data Models list indicating acceleration is active.

integrity.description: Agent used the Splunk Web UI to create the data model
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the agent navigating the Splunk web interface in a browser at localhost:8000, rather than just writing configuration files in a terminal or text editor.

integrity.description: Agent interacted with the actual Splunk application
integrity.visual_evidence: The interface should be the genuine Splunk Enterprise dashboard, not a mock-up, and should not show signs of HTML manipulation (like Inspect Element) to fake the data model name or settings.

integrity.description: Agent did not just type the expected answers into a text file
integrity.visual_evidence: The screenshots must show the actual Splunk Data Model editor with the constraints and fields properly configured in the UI forms, not just a text document containing the task description's keywords.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The data model must be named exactly 'Authentication_Events'. The root dataset must be constrained to 'index=security_logs'. The model must contain at least 2 child objects (datasets) and at least 3 defined fields. Data model acceleration must be enabled.
